title: Crowd-sourced automated review of forensic files
abstract: Provided herein is a system and method for review of forensic report computer files. The method can involve notifying a plurality of service providers of a job to be performed, accepting a bid from a service provider to perform the job, providing the computer file to the service provider, receiving from the service provider a forensic result such as a reviewed file.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED: 1. A automated forensic data analysis system enabled for crowd-sourcing expert review comprising: a logic processor with a memory or storage configured with logic instructions to provide or facilitate: a data-accessing module for reading and reviewing an original forensic biological data file; an evaluation module for determining whether the original forensic biological data file meets quality criteria for uploading to a forensic matching or identification service or database; a crowd-sourcing expert review module for communicating with one or more experts for review of the data file when quality criteria or not met and receiving notifications and results from the one or more experts. 2. The system of claim 1 further comprising: a reprocessing communication module for requesting or initiating reprocessing of a sample when quality criteria for the file are not met; and a user interface module for communicating processing progress and processing results to a user and for receiving configuration data from an administrative user requiring quality criteria and the one or more experts. 3. A crowd-sourcing server for providing rapid expert review of forensic data files for a plurality of customers using a plurality of independent forensic expert services comprising: a logic processor with a memory or storage configured with logic instructions to provide or facilitate: a customer interface module for communicating with customers needing rapid expert review of forensic data files not meeting quality criteria for automated analysis; a service provider module for communicating with a plurality of forensic experts or forensic expert services providing review of forensic data files; a matching module for matching service providers with a data file requiring review and communicating availability of a review job to the selected experts; and 

 a job management module for receiving bids from service providers for a job, assigning an expert to a job, communicating job details to the expert, confirming that results received from the expert meet job criteria. 4. A method comprising: a) providing to a plurality of forensic service providers, over a communications network, notification of a job to be performed, wherein the job comprises reviewing one or more forensic data files and delivering a forensic result;; b) receiving from one or more service providers, over a communications network, a response indicating willingness to perform the job; c) selecting a service provider to perform the job based on at least one selection criterion; d) providing access to one or more forensic data files, over a communications network, to the selected service provider; and e) receiving from the selected service provider, over a communications network, a forensic result. 5. The method of claim 4 further wherein: the job comprises accessing an original STR file to be reviewed and providing a reviewed and optionally revised STR profile computer file or a determination that the original file can or cannot be used for further processing. 6. A method comprising: a) accessing data regarding one or more of service providers qualified to perform a job, wherein the job comprises completing review of one or more forensic data files ; b) selecting one or more service providers to request performance of the job; c) providing to one or more selected service providers, over a communications network, notification of the job and optionally access to the original file or other data to complete the job; d) receiving from one or more service providers, over a communications network, one or more responses regarding the job; e) upon job completion by at least one service provider, taking a further action. 7. The method of claim 6 further wherein: 

 the job comprises accessing an original STR file to be reviewed and providing a reviewed and optionally revised STR profile computer file or a determination that the original file can or cannot be used for further processing; and the further action comprises uploading a reviewed file to a forensic database. 8. The method of claim 4 further comprising, before (a), the step of receiving from a customer: (i) a request to have the job performed and (ii) one or more forensic data files. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the customer is a law enforcement agency or organ thereof. 10. The method of claim 4 wherein the notification is provided over a web-based application, by email, by text message, by SMS notification or by telephone. 11. The method of claim 4 wherein all steps are computer implemented. 12. The method of claim 4 wherein the notification is provided to any of a hand-held mobile device, a computer tablet, a laptop computer, a desktop computer or a television. 13. The method of claim 4 wherein the notification is withdrawn after a service provider is selected or after a predetermined amount of time. 14. The method of claim 4 wherein the one or more responses are provided over a web- based application, by email, by text message, by SMS notification or by telephone. 15. The method of claim 4 comprising re-providing the notification if no response is received within a pre-determined amount of time. 16. The method of claim 4 wherein the service provider selected is the first to provide a response. 17. The method of claim 4 wherein the service provider is licensed to perform the job. 18. The method of claim 4 wherein the service provider is selected based, at least in part, on a quality score assigned to the service provider. 19. The method of claim 18 wherein the quality score comprises a measure of any of physical location, speed of response, quality of response, and cost. 20. The method of claim 4 wherein the one or more forensic data files comprise an annotated electropherogram of STR amplicons. 21. The method of claim 4 wherein the one or more forensic data files comprise STR allele calls. 22. The method of claim 4 wherein the one or more forensic data files comprise at least one flag indicating at least one item failing to pass a confidence threshold for identification. 23. The method of claim 22 wherein the one or more forensic data files comprise STR data files and the at least one flag indicates an off-ladder peak, an allele imbalance, a pull- up, or an out of bin peak. 24. The method of claim 4 wherein the selected service provider accesses or takes delivery of the one or more files by clicking an access icon on a computing device. 25. The method of claim 4 wherein the one or more files or a link thereto is delivered to the service provider by email. 26. The method of claim 4 wherein the forensic results comprise a reviewed STR profile computer file with at least one flag cleared from the STR profile computer file delivered to the service provider. 27. The method of claim 4 wherein a system administrator performs a quality check of the reviewed STR profile computer file. 28. The method of claim 4 wherein the job is withdrawn from a selected service provider if delivery of the forensic results is not performed within any of 1 minute, 10 minutes, 1 hour, 3 hours, 12 hours or 24 hours. 29. The method of claim 4 further comprising compensating the service provider for performing the j ob . 30. The method of claim 5 further comprising: f) converting the reviewed STR profile computer file into a forensic DNA database- compatible computer file and uploading the compatible file to a forensic DNA database. 31. The method of claim 30 wherein the compatible file is a CODIS file. 32. The method of claim 4 further comprising any combination of the steps of claims 5 to the proceeding claim. 33. A method comprising: a) receiving from a system administrator, over a communications network, notification of a job to be performed, wherein the job comprises delivering a reviewed STR profile computer file to the system administrator; b) providing to the system administrator, over a communications network, a response indicating willingness to perform the job; c) taking delivery from the system administrator, over a communications network, an STR profile computer file to be reviewed; d) reviewing the STR profile computer file to remove at least one flagged item in the STR profile computer file, thereby generating a reviewed STR profile computer file; and e) delivering to the system administrator, over a communications network, the reviewed STR profile computer file. 34. A system comprising: a) at least one network interface; b) at least one processor to execute software; c) software which, when executed by the at least one processor, performs the steps of claim 4. 35. The system of claim 34 comprising a database of service providers. 36. The system of claim 34 further comprising a sub-system configured to perform rapid Human ID. 37. The system of claim 34 comprising software which, when executed by the at least one processor, performs the steps of claim 4 and any combination of claims 5 to claim 31 and claims 35 and 36. 38. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium storing instructions that, when executed by one or more processors, performs the steps of claim 4. 39. The storage medium of claim 38 including instructions that, when executed by one or more processor, performs the steps of claim 4 and any combination of claims 5 to claim 31. 40. A method of performing a biological analysis comprising: a) accessing or receiving an automatically generated biological analysis file; b) determining that the file requires confirmation or otherwise does not meet quality criteria; c) automatically initiating a confirmation for the file; d) automatically receiving confirmation results; and e) transmitting a biological analysis file in accordance with the confirmation results. 41. The method of claim 40 wherein the confirmation comprise one or more of: a request that a biological analysis file be regenerated by repeating the biological analysis; and a request that a biological analysis file be presented to a human evaluator for review. 42. The method of claim 40 wherein c) further comprises: automatically communicating a notification to one or more human reviewers that a request for review of a file is pending. 43. The method of claim 42 further comprising: receiving and automatically reviewing one or more responses to the request from human reviewers; selecting one or more human reviewers to review the file; assigning the file to one or more human reviewers; transmitting the plurality of files to assigned reviewers; and receiving correction or confirmation results from assigned reviewers. 44. The method of claim 40 wherein the biological analysis file comprises automatically generated STR profile computer files. 45. The method of claim 40, wherein the computer system is a biological analysis system of a law enforcement agency or organ thereof. 46. The method of claim 40 wherein determining comprises determining whether the file satisfies selected quality criteria. 47. The method of claim 40 comprising executing a software-implemented protocol having parameters that can be set by a human system administrator. 48. The method of claim 47 wherein the parameters include criteria for determining when the file requires further confirmation; criteria for determining when a file requiring further confirmation is to be regenerated or sent to a human evaluator for review; criteria for selecting a human evaluator; and/or time limits for receiving further confirmation results. 49. The method of claim 40 wherein the request is made to a service that pairs the file with at least one human evaluator. 50. The method of claim 40 wherein the request is made to one or more pre-selected human evaluators. 51. The method of claim 42 wherein the request notification is transmitted via a web- based application, by email, by text message, by SMS notification or by telephone. 52. The method of claim 40 wherein all steps are computer implemented. 53. The method of claim 42 wherein the request notification is provided over any of a hand-held mobile device, a computer tablet, a laptop computer, a desktop computer or a television. 54. The method of claim 42 wherein the notification is withdrawn after a service provider is selected or after a predetermined amount of time. 55. The method of claim 42 wherein the one or more responses are provided over a web- based application, by email, by text message, by SMS notification or by telephone. 56. The method of claim 42 comprising re-providing the notification if no response is received within a pre-determined amount of time. 57. The method of claim 42 wherein the reviewer selected is the first to provide a response. 58. The method of claim 42 wherein the reviewer is licensed to perform the review. 59. The method of claim 42 wherein the reviewer is selected based, at least in part, on a quality score assigned to the reviewer. 60. The method of claim 54 wherein the quality score comprises a measure of any of physical location, speed of response and quality of response. 61. The method of claim 44 wherein the STR computer file comprises an annotated electropherogram of STR amplicons. 62. The method of claim 44 wherein the STR computer file comprises STR allele calls. 63. The method of claim 44 wherein the STR computer file comprise at least one flag indicating at least one item failing to pass a confidence threshold for identification. 64. The method of claim 63 wherein at least one flag indicates an off-ladder peak, an allele imbalance, a pull-up, or an out of bin peak. 65. The method of claim 42 wherein the selected reviewer takes delivery of biological analysis computer file by clicking an access icon on a computing device. 66. The method of claim 44 wherein the STR computer file is delivered to the service provider by email. 67. The method of claim 44 wherein the reviewed STR profile computer file comprises at least one flag cleared from the STR profile computer file delivered to the service provider. 68. The method of claim 44 wherein a the computer system performs a quality check of the reviewed STR profile computer file. 69. The method of claim 42 wherein the job is withdrawn from a selected reviewer if delivery of the reviewed STR profile computer file is not taken within any of 1 minute, 10 minutes, 1 hour, 3 hours, 12 hours or 24 hours. 70. The method of claim 42 further comprising compensating a reviewer for performing the review. 71. The method of claim 42 further comprising: f) converting the reviewed STR profile computer file into a forensic DNA database- compatible computer file and uploading the compatible file to a forensic DNA database. 72. The method of claim 66 wherein the compatible file is a CODIS file. 73. The method of claim 40 and any combination of claims 41 to the proceeding claim. 74. A system comprising: a) at least one network interface; b) at least one processor to execute software; c) software which, when executed by the at least one processor, performs the steps of claim 40. 75. The system of claim 68 comprising a database of service providers. 76. The system of claim 68 further comprising a sub-system configured to perform rapid Human ID. 77. The system of claim 74 including software that, when executed by one or more processor, performs the steps of claim 40 and any combination of claims 41 to claim 72 and 75 to 76. 78. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium storing instructions that, when executed by one or more processors, performs the steps of claim 40. 79. The storage medium of claim 78 including instructions that, when executed by one or more processor, performs the steps of claim 4 and any combination of claims 41 to claim 72.
